report_name: New 'GodDamn' Ransomware Targets Windows Systems With Stealthy Approach

threat_actors.name: GodDamn operators
threat_actors.description: The operators of the GodDamn ransomware, who use stealthy techniques and provide multiple communication channels for extortion.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The GodDamn ransomware encrypts user and system files, renaming them with a victim-specific ID and the .God8Damn extension.

attack_patterns.name: File and Directory Discovery
attack_patterns.description: The malware systematically scans the file system for user and system files to encrypt.

attack_patterns.name: Masquerading
attack_patterns.description: The malware was designed to mimic the behavior of legitimate software to evade detection and prolong its presence on a system before initiating encryption.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The ransomware deletes Volume Shadow Copies using 'vssadmin.exe delete shadows' to prevent easy file recovery.

attack_patterns.name: File Deletion
attack_patterns.description: The ransomware deletes original files after encrypting them to hinder recovery.

malware.name: GodDamn
malware.description: A new ransomware strain that targets Windows systems, encrypts files appending the .God8Damn extension, and drops a ransom note named README.TXT. It is designed to be stealthy to prolong dwell time.

tools.name: vssadmin
tools.description: Used to delete Volume Shadow Copies to inhibit system recovery from ransomware.

identities.name: Windows operating systems
identities.description: The target operating system for the GodDamn ransomware, noted for being ubiquitous in corporate environments.

infrastructure.name: GodDamn Communication Channels
infrastructure.description: The ransomware operators provide multiple communication channels, including email and a messaging ID, for ransom negotiation.

indicators.name: GodDamn Ransom Note File
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: GodDamn Ransomware Ransom Note
id: 2b3c4d5e-6f7a-4b8c-9d0e-1f2a3b4c5d6e
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of the GodDamn ransomware ransom note 'README.TXT'.
references:
  - https://www.cyfirma.com/news/weekly-intelligence-report-19-jun-2026/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-19
tags:
  - attack.t1486
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\README.TXT'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of the 'README.TXT' ransom note associated with GodDamn ransomware.

indicators.name: GodDamn Ransomware File Encryption Pattern
indicators.pattern:
title: GodDamn Ransomware File Encryption
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-4a90-8bcd-ef1234567890
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of files with the .God8Damn extension, which is indicative of the GodDamn ransomware encrypting files.
references:
  - https://www.cyfirma.com/news/weekly-intelligence-report-19-jun-2026/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-19
tags:
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.impact
level: critical
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.God8Damn'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects files being renamed with the '.God8Damn' extension, a key indicator of GodDamn ransomware activity.

indicators.name: GodDamn Volume Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: GodDamn Ransomware VSS Deletion
id: 9a8b7c6d-5e4f-4a32-1bcd-ef0987654321
status: experimental
description: Detects the command used by GodDamn ransomware to delete Volume Shadow Copies.
references:
  - https://www.cyfirma.com/news/weekly-intelligence-report-19-jun-2026/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-19
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete shadow copies, a common ransomware technique to inhibit recovery.
